summary: AgentForge v4.5 Build Farm testing complete. All 20 backend API tests passed (100%). Build Farm Panel UI works correctly with real-time polling. 3D Map has WebGL/R3F rendering issue causing error boundary fallback.

ui_bugs.issue: React Three Fiber (R3F) throws error: 'Cannot set x-line-number' causing WebGL context loss
ui_bugs.notes: Error occurs during Canvas unmount/remount. Error boundary added to catch gracefully.

passed_tests: ✅ GET /api/build-farm/status - returns total_workers, active_workers, queued_jobs, running_jobs, completed_jobs, failed_jobs, active_builds
✅ GET /api/build-farm/workers - returns list of workers with id, name, status, capabilities
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/add - adds job to queue with correct status='queued'
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/add - supports all job types (prototype, full_build, demo, code_gen, test_suite)
✅ GET /api/build-farm/jobs - returns jobs list sorted by priority
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/start - starts job and assigns worker (or returns 400 if no workers)
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/start - returns 404 for nonexistent job
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/cancel - cancels queued job successfully
✅ POST /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/cancel - returns 404 for nonexistent job
✅ GET /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/logs - returns logs, status, progress
✅ GET /api/build-farm/jobs/{id}/logs - returns 404 for nonexistent job
✅ POST /api/build-farm/workers/{id}/pause - pauses worker
✅ POST /api/build-farm/workers/{id}/resume - resumes paused worker
✅ Full job lifecycle test (add → start → monitor)
✅ Frontend BuildFarmPanel - data-testid='build-farm-panel' renders
✅ Frontend BuildFarmPanel - shows 'Distributed Build Farm' header
✅ Frontend BuildFarmPanel - shows workers grid with stats (3 Workers, Building, Queued, Complete, Failed)
✅ Frontend BuildFarmPanel - 'Add Build Job' section with job type buttons
✅ Frontend BuildFarmPanel - 'Queue Prototype' button works and shows toast 'Job added to build farm!'
✅ Frontend CommandCenter - 12 sub-tabs rendered (Mission, Goal Loop, Idea→Reality, Knowledge, Autopsy, Debug, Time, Ideas, SaaS, Farm, Agents+, 3D Map)
✅ Frontend CommandCenter - Farm tab (cmd-farm-tab) accessible and shows BuildFarmPanel
✅ Frontend CommandCenter - 3D Map tab (cmd-viz-tab) accessible
action_items: FIX: SystemVisualization3D - R3F error 'Cannot set x-line-number' during Canvas unmount. Consider using frameloop='demand' or lazy loading the Canvas component
OPTIONAL: Investigate react-three/fiber compatibility with tab switching in React
critical_code_review_comments: Build Farm backend is well-implemented with simulated build stages (Setup, Code Gen, Asset Processing, Testing, Packaging)
BuildFarmPanel has proper real-time polling (2s interval) for status updates
SystemVisualization3D has React Three Fiber Canvas rendering issue - error boundary added but underlying R3F reconciler issue needs deeper fix
seed_data_creation: Created test projects via API for job testing (TEST_BuildFarmProject, TEST_LifecycleProject)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Build Farm APIs fully functional and tested. BuildFarmPanel UI works correctly with add job, workers grid, job queue, and real-time polling. The 3D Map (SystemVisualization3D) has a React Three Fiber rendering issue causing 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' error. Error boundary was added but the underlying issue is in the R3F reconciler during Canvas unmount/remount when switching tabs. This may require using lazy loading or frameloop='demand' to fix properly.

rca_of_issue.issue: SystemVisualization3D 3D Map crashes with R3F error
rca_of_issue.root_cause: React Three Fiber reconciler has known issues with Canvas unmount during tab switching. The error 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' occurs when the reconciler tries to clean up Three.js objects that have already been disposed.
reproduction_steps: 4. Error appears in console and error overlay
mitigation_attempted: Added CanvasErrorBoundary to catch errors gracefully
Changed Line component to native Three.js line
Added canvasReady state to delay Canvas mounting
rca_of_issue.recommended_fix: Use Suspense and lazy loading for the Canvas component, or implement frameloop='demand' to reduce render loop issues. May also need to upgrade @react-three/fiber to latest version.
